meta.title: Attachment style (ECR-RS)
meta.subtitle: How you relate and bond in close relationships.
meta.description: Nine statements about your close relationships in general, measuring two attachment dimensions: avoidance (discomfort with closeness and depending on others) and anxiety (worry about being cared for). Answer thinking about the people who are close to you overall.
meta.disclaimer: This assessment supports self-reflection and does not provide a clinical diagnosis.

consent.title: Consent and Disclaimer
consent.body: I understand this is a self-reflection tool, not a clinical test, and I consent to store results locally on this device.

interpretation.low: Lower on this attachment dimension — a sign of greater security here.

items.ecrrs_01: It helps to turn to people who are close to me in times of need.
items.ecrrs_02: I usually discuss my problems and concerns with people who are close to me.
items.ecrrs_03: I talk things over with people who are close to me.
items.ecrrs_04: I find it easy to depend on people who are close to me.
items.ecrrs_05: I don't feel comfortable opening up to others.
items.ecrrs_06: I prefer not to show others how I feel deep down.
items.ecrrs_07: I often worry that people who are close to me don't really care for me.
items.ecrrs_08: I'm afraid that people who are close to me may abandon me.
items.ecrrs_09: I worry that others won't care about me as much as I care about them.
